## ⚙️ Module Functions

### Module-Level Functions

#### lurek.agent.new

#### Definition

```lua
--- Creates a new LLM Agent instance. The configuration table must specify at least the endpoint URL, model, format (json or text) and optional prompt parameters.
---@param config table Table containing 'url', 'model', 'system_prompt', 'format' and 'options' subtable.
---@return LAgent A new agent object.
lurek.agent.new = function(config) end
```

#### Description

Creates a new LLM Agent instance. The configuration table must specify at least the endpoint URL, model, format (json or text) and optional prompt parameters.

Parameters:

- `config` (`table`, required): Table containing 'url', 'model', 'system_prompt', 'format' and 'options' subtable.

Returns: `LAgent` - A new agent object.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({
        url = "http://localhost:11434/api/generate",
        model = "llama3",
        system_prompt = "You are a helpful AI.",
        format = "json",
        options = {
            num_ctx = 4096,
            temperature = 0.7
        }
    })
    print("Agent created!")
end
```

#### lurek.agent.newManager

#### Definition

```lua
--- Creates a new Agent Manager for batching and handling multiple LLM agents across a shared thread pool.
---@return LAgentManager A new agent manager object.
lurek.agent.newManager = function() end
```

#### Description

Creates a new Agent Manager for batching and handling multiple LLM agents across a shared thread pool.

Returns: `LAgentManager` - A new agent manager object.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local manager = lurek.agent.newManager()
    print("Agent manager created!")
end
```

## 🔷 Module Types

### LAgent

#### Description

Lua-side handle for a single LLM Agent.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({
        url = "http://localhost:11434/api/generate",
        model = "llama3",
        system_prompt = "You are a helpful AI.",
        format = "json",
        options = {
            num_ctx = 4096,
            temperature = 0.7
        }
    })
    print("Agent created!")
end
```

### LAgentManager

#### Description

Lua-side handle for managing multiple LLM Agents in parallel.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua

    local manager = lurek.agent.newManager()
    print("Agent manager created!")
end
```

## 🔹 Module Methods

### LAgent Methods

#### LAgent:addSkill

#### Definition

```lua
--- Adds a skill to the agent's context.
---@param name string The name of the skill.
---@param prompt string The instructional prompt for the skill.
---@return nil No value is returned.
function LAgent:addSkill(name, prompt) end
```

#### Description

Adds a skill to the agent's context.

Parameters:

- `name` (`string`, required): The name of the skill.
- `prompt` (`string`, required): The instructional prompt for the skill.

Returns: - No value is returned.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({})
    agent:addSkill("location", "The player is currently in a forest.")
    print("Skill added to agent context.")
end
```

#### LAgent:evalCode

#### Definition

```lua
--- Evaluates Lua code securely within the VM.
---@param code string The Lua code to execute.
---@return boolean True on success.
function LAgent:evalCode(code) end
```

#### Description

Evaluates Lua code securely within the VM.

Parameters:

- `code` (`string`, required): The Lua code to execute.

Returns: `boolean` - True on success.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({})
    local success, err = agent:evalCode("local x = 10; print('Eval x: ', x)")
    if success then
        print("evalCode executed successfully.")
    else
        print("evalCode failed: ", err)
    end
end
```

#### LAgent:prompt

#### Definition

```lua
--- Sends an instructional prompt to the LLM asynchronously.
---@param instruction string The specific task instruction for the agent.
---@param callback function Function called with `(success, data, err_info)` when complete.
---@return number Callback ID.
function LAgent:prompt(instruction, callback) end
```

#### Description

Sends an instructional prompt to the LLM asynchronously.

Parameters:

- `instruction` (`string`, required): The specific task instruction for the agent.
- `callback` (`function`, required): Function called with `(success, data, err_info)` when complete.

Returns: `integer` - Callback ID.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({
        url = "http://localhost:11434/api/generate",
        model = "llama3",
        format = "json"
    })
    
    -- Note: This is an async call. In a real game, you must call agent:update() in the game loop to poll responses.
    agent:prompt("Who are you?", function(success, data, err_info)
        if success then
            print("Response: ", data.response)
        else
            print("Error: ", err_info.message)
        end
    end)
    print("Prompt dispatched.")
end
```

#### LAgent:promptBatch

#### Definition

```lua
--- Sends a batch of prompts to the LLM asynchronously.
---@param instructions table List of instructions.
---@param callback function Function called with `(results_table)` when all complete.
---@return number Callback ID.
function LAgent:promptBatch(instructions, callback) end
```

#### Description

Sends a batch of prompts to the LLM asynchronously.

Parameters:

- `instructions` (`table`, required): List of instructions.
- `callback` (`function`, required): Function called with `(results_table)` when all complete.

Returns: `integer` - Callback ID.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({
        url = "http://localhost:11434/api/generate",
        model = "llama3",
        format = "json"
    })
    
    agent:promptBatch({
        "Describe the bridge.",
        "Describe the engine room."
    }, function(results)
        for i, res in ipairs(results) do
            if res.success then
                print("Result " .. i .. ": ", res.data.description)
            else
                print("Task " .. i .. " failed: ", res.error.message)
            end
        end
    end)
    print("Batch dispatched.")
end
```

#### LAgent:update

#### Definition

```lua
--- Polls the background client for completed LLM requests.
---@return nil No value is returned.
function LAgent:update() end
```

#### Description

Polls the background client for completed LLM requests.

Returns: - No value is returned.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local agent = lurek.agent.new({})
    agent:update()
    print("Agent polled for responses.")
end
```

### LAgentManager Methods

#### LAgentManager:runAll

#### Definition

```lua
--- Runs multiple agent tasks in parallel.
---@param tasks table A table of `{ agent = LuaAgent, instruction = "..." }`.
---@param callback function Function called with a table of results when ALL tasks finish.
---@return number Callback ID.
function LAgentManager:runAll(tasks, callback) end
```

#### Description

Runs multiple agent tasks in parallel.

Parameters:

- `tasks` (`table`, required): A table of `{ agent = LuaAgent, instruction = "..." }`.
- `callback` (`function`, required): Function called with a table of results when ALL tasks finish.

Returns: `integer` - Callback ID.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local manager = lurek.agent.newManager()
    manager:runAll()
    print("Manager runAll triggered.")
end
```

#### LAgentManager:update

#### Definition

```lua
--- Polls the manager's background client for completed tasks.
---@return nil No value is returned.
function LAgentManager:update() end
```

#### Description

Polls the manager's background client for completed tasks.

Returns: - No value is returned.

#### Example

Source: [agent.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/agent.lua)

```lua
do
    local manager = lurek.agent.newManager()
    manager:update()
    print("Manager polled for all batch responses.")
end
```
